title: Cheirostylis glandulifera
intro: Cheirostylis glandulifera, also known as the glandular Cheirostylis, is a species of flowering plant in the family Berberidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce oxidative stress, alleviate pain and inflammation, and prevent infections. The bioactive constituents of C. glandulifera include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, including glycosides, phenolic acids, and other secondary metabolites that contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, C. glandulifera is often prepared as a decoction or a powder, and is used in various herbal preparations, including capsules, tablets, and infusions, to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Cheirostylis glandulifera is classified as a species within the genus Cheirostylis, which belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family is further classified under the order Asparagales and the division Magnoliophyta. Cheirostylis glandulifera is a terrestrial orchid species. The family Orchidaceae comprises a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The classification of Cheirostylis glandulifera reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other orchid species.
uses: Cheirostylis glandulifera is said to have various medicinal properties. It is reported to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which can help in treating infections and skin problems.
